tn_central_transfers.note: TN's share in central tax pool has declined from ~6% (2000s) to ~3.7% under 15th Finance Commission formula — a long-standing grievance since higher population growth states gain more under population-based devolution.

gst_data.note: GST compensation to states ended in June 2022. TN was a net loser in GST transition as it had a high manufacturing base and GST shifted tax collection to consumption states.
gst_data.tn_loss_in_gst_transition_analysis: TN being a producing state historically collected more sales tax. Under GST (destination-based), revenue shifted partially to consuming states. PTR (FM under DMK) estimated TN's annual loss at ~₹8,000-12,000 crore post-compensation period.

14th_fc.key_formula_weights: Population 2011: 17.5%, Area: 15%, Forest cover: 7.5%, Income distance: 50%, DTCP efficiency: 10%

15th_fc.key_formula_weights: Population 2011: 15%, Area: 15%, Forest & Ecology: 10%, Income distance: 45%, Tax effort & Demographic performance: 15%
15th_fc.note: TN benefited slightly from demographic performance criterion (lower fertility rate) but lost ground due to relative income distance formula.

composite_water_management_index.note: Chennai Day Zero crisis (2019) highlighted urban water vulnerability despite relatively good score

aspirational_districts.note: Tamil Nadu has no aspirational districts — all districts meet baseline development thresholds.
